text:
Hey, I'm really sorry you're going through this. Breakups are genuinely painful, and there's no way around that — you just have to move through it, and that's hard.

Whatever you're feeling right now is okay. If you're sad, that makes sense. If you're angry, that makes sense too. Even if you feel relieved in some moments and then devastated in the next, that's completely normal. Grief over a relationship doesn't follow a straight line.

I know it can be tempting to replay everything — to look for the moment it went wrong, to wonder what you could have done differently, to go back over old messages or memories. That's a very human thing to do, but try not to be too hard on yourself while you do it. Sometimes relationships end not because anyone failed, but simply because two people weren't right for each other, or because the timing was wrong, or because life pulled in different directions. It doesn't always mean something went wrong with you.

The days ahead might feel really long. That's okay. You don't have to have it all figured out. You don't have to be "over it" on anyone else's schedule. Let yourself eat the comfort food, watch the movie you love, call the people who make you feel safe. The small things genuinely help more than they seem like they should.

And here's what I truly believe: you will feel better. Not because the pain isn't real, but because you are more resilient than you know. People who have felt exactly this way — gutted, uncertain, exhausted — have come out the other side and found their footing again. You will too.

You don't have to carry this alone. I'm here.
